item.title: Facilitating sensitivity analysis of hydrological models through knowledge-driven configuration and distributed online model services
item.description: This work studies how configuration knowledge and service-oriented model execution can be combined to support more efficient sensitivity analysis for hydrological models.

item.title: Construction of an open knowledge framework for geoscientific models
item.description: The paper presents a three-level framework covering model resources, their related materials, and practical application knowledge, with OpenGMS as an implementation case.

item.title: Retrieval of Fine-Grained PM2.5 Spatiotemporal Resolution Based on Multiple Machine Learning Models
item.description: This article evaluates multiple machine learning algorithms for PM2.5 estimation and shows how multi-source urban data can improve fine-grained spatiotemporal retrieval.
